title: Title-fallback path is lossy and its ledger under-reports severity (root cause of DEF-002/003/004). _map_register_row falls back to _clean_line (caps 120 chars, strips every ASCII hyphen) and then writes statement: title, so the fallback rows' full text is destroyed rather than merely their title. Named 131 rows on this package: FR 78, TEST 50, PH 4.
custom_attributes: {"ref": "findings_4.md D-0", "source": "migrate.py:147-149,247-254", "recoverable_from_package": false, "note": "findings_3 B4 asked for this ledger to be grouped because it was noisy; grouping a data-loss signal makes it quieter, not safer"}

title: Silent truncation of roughly 415 fields at exactly 120 or 200 characters, with no warning at preview, confirm, fidelity or gate stage. Unrecoverable for 78 requirements.statement and 133 wbs_items.title; cosmetic elsewhere because statement retains the full text.

Hyphen deletion corrupts governed identifiers. The _clean_line character class removes every ASCII hyphen, turning FR-001 into FR 001 and severing BL- cross-references. Zero of 155 wbs_items titles contain a hyphen against a source full of them.

Typed-column starvation: values parked in custom_attributes.v1 while the typed column was left NULL. Affects tests.kind (50), deferred_work.activation_trigger (23), kpis.measure (21), wbs_items.phase_id (155), stakeholders.role (6). Invisible to every existing gate.
custom_attributes: {"ref": "findings_4.md D-3", "repair_task": "Task 6", "recoverable_from_package": true, "note": "stakeholders.role is vacuous - the v1 value is byte-identical to name - so it is recorded here rather than written"}

title: Positional identifier renumbering. D-nn was mapped to DW-NNN by on-disk row order over an unsorted source, so five ids silently shifted: DW-015 is D-16, DW-016 is D-17, DW-017 is D-18, DW-018 is D-19, DW-019 is D-15, DW-020 is D-21, DW-021 is D-22, DW-022 is D-20. Every D-nn reference in the progress log, ADRs and commits now resolves to the wrong row.
custom_attributes: {"ref": "findings_4.md D-4", "repair_task": "Task 3.3 + 3.4", "recoverable_from_package": true, "note": "ids are FK-referenced primary keys and cannot be renumbered; mitigated by title prefix plus crosswalk, not undone"}

title: Degenerate titles on the 20 WBS epic rows: each took the phase-number cell as its title, so WBS-1.title was the string 1 and WBS-11.title was 1 to 2. The real epic names sat unused in custom_attributes.v1.Title.

A source document was consumed into rows and its narrative discarded. planning/roadmap.md produced 4 phase rows and 6 milestone rows and no narrative document, losing every phase's Goal, Scope, Deliverables, Validation and Exit gate prose, the authoritative P1 to P19 build-slice ladder, and the Legacy token map that disambiguates three colliding P-number schemes.

Section splitting on level-two headings only. The progress log switched to flat level-three dated entries partway through, so 23 dated entries covering P15 through P19 collapsed into a single 82622-character Preamble section - the most recent history became the least navigable.

Emitted handoff prompts are not re-anchored and contradict the cutover. Relative links written for docs/handoff/ are all dead at repo root and one resolves to the wrong file; the bodies instruct running the v1 validator on the frozen archive and hand-editing frozen registers; and the kickoff prompt AGENTS.md points at is seven slices stale, naming a slice the operator deferred indefinitely.

Status defaulting applied where the status is present. All four phases defaulted to Approved because the roadmap phase table has no status column, but the status sits in the prose immediately beneath it: complete for PH-0 and PH-1, substantially delivered for PH-2, not started for PH-3.

Risk status written to the wrong column. The v1 Status values landed in lifecycle_status while the purpose-built risk_state kept its schema default, so risk_state read open on all 11 rows and a Closed risk and an Accepted risk were both reported as open.

The derived backlog view is a false register. All 155 wbs_items migrated as Approved, and v_backlog selects everything not Implemented, Superseded, Obsolete or Rejected, so it reports 155 open work items for a project whose ladder is complete.

Ordering bug creates a permanent structural dead-end. Acceptance criteria were bumped to Approved on import, before any slice existed, and the immutability trigger then forbids changing slice_id on an Approved criterion. Every acceptance criterion is therefore Approved with slice_id NULL and can never be bound to a slice, so v_phase_exit returns zero for every phase permanently. Not fixable after cutover: the only legal path is superseding all 74 criteria, refused as disproportionate for a retrospective view of four closed phases. The upstream fix is to leave imported criteria Proposed until after any slice binding.
custom_attributes: {"ref": "findings_4.md D-12", "repair_task": "none - unfixable after cutover", "recoverable_from_package": false, "note": "the only legal path is superseding all 74 criteria; refused as disproportionate for a retrospective view of closed phases"}
